meta.title: MergeCore Tauri Rules
meta.description: Rules for Tauri apps (Rust backend + webview frontend): capabilities, IPC commands, shell and filesystem use, and update channel hygiene.
meta.notes: Gate with tauri: true. Targets Tauri 2.x; some rules flag 1.x allowlist usage as deprecated in new code.

scoring.normalisation: Per review unit (one Rust command module, one capability/permission file, or one webview entry), sum penalties after optional quality_signal multiplier, then score = max(0, min(10, initial_score - min(sum_penalties, max_total_penalty_per_file))).

interpretation.9_to_10: Narrow capabilities, validated IPC, no raw shell.
interpretation.7_to_8: Mergeable; tighten one permission or command validator.
interpretation.5_to_6: Risky: wide fs/shell scope, unvalidated command args.
interpretation.0_to_4: Block: arbitrary shell exec, permissive CSP, or globbed fs access.

rules.title: Overly broad filesystem scope
rules.description: Granting $HOME/**/* or similar to fs permissions gives the webview access far beyond the app's needs. Scope to specific directories.

rules.title: shell.execute with user-controlled command
rules.description: Allowing shell execution with arbitrary commands or templated args from the webview is remote code execution. Use shell.scope allowlist with fixed commands, or avoid shell entirely.

heuristics: Command path or args constructed from frontend input

rules.title: Permissive Content Security Policy
rules.description: CSP with unsafe-inline, unsafe-eval, or wildcard sources defeats the webview isolation boundary.

rules.title: Tauri command without argument validation
rules.description: #[tauri::command] functions should validate or parse inputs; trusting the frontend blindly defeats the backend privilege separation.

heuristics: Command takes String or PathBuf parameters and uses them unvalidated

examples.before: #[tauri::command] fn open_file(path: String) -> Result<String, String> { std::fs::read_to_string(&path).map_err(|e| e.to_string()) }
examples.after: validate path is inside app_data_dir before reading

rules.title: Updater without signature verification
rules.description: Disabling or omitting update signature keys lets a compromised endpoint deliver arbitrary binaries to users.

rules.title: Capability applied to all windows
rules.description: Granting sensitive permissions via a capability with "windows": ["main", ...] to every surface increases blast radius. Scope to the windows that actually need the feature.

rules.title: Untyped invoke payloads on the frontend
rules.description: invoke<T>('cmd', args) with weak types at the boundary leads to runtime surprises. Define a shared schema and narrow on receipt.

rules.title: Path joined from frontend without canonicalisation
rules.description: Joining user-supplied segments onto a base dir allows .. traversal. Canonicalise and check the result is inside the allowed base.

rules.title: Debug logs leak to production builds
rules.description: Leaving noisy log::debug! or println! on user data in shipped builds is a support and privacy liability. Use log level filtering or feature flags.

rules.title: Strong pattern: least-privilege capability set
rules.description: Capability files that enable only the specific permissions a window uses (e.g. fs:allow-read-file for one scope) show disciplined hardening.
